upgradePromptPrefix: We are in the process of doing an upgrade of the Self-Managed Commerce code base from version {FROM_VERSION} to version {TO_VERSION}.
patchConsumptionPromptPrefix: We are in the process of consuming the latest patches from the Self-Managed Commerce code base version {TO_VERSION}.

steps.title: Resolve remaining Git merge conflicts
steps.prompt: Review the merge conflicts in the current folder and help to address them. "ours" represents the customer's custom code base, and "theirs" represents the new Self-Managed Commerce release code. IMPORTANT: Ensure that you do not overwrite "ours" changes with "theirs" unless the differences don't affect functionality. Make sure to check each file manually - don't simply replace with "theirs" unless the changes are whitespace or no longer needed due to the platform changes. Assume that files deleted in "theirs" were removed intentionally and accept the deletion. Accept the new "theirs" versions for all dependency upgrades. Continue working until all Git merge conflicts are resolved. We do not need to run Maven commands to check if the code compiles in this step.

steps.title: Resolve all compilation issues
steps.prompt: Run the validation command below and help to fix any compilation issues. The upgrade notes at https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/{TO_VERSION}/upgrade-notes.html, https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/{TO_VERSION}/release-notes.html#upgrade-notes, or https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/upgrade-notes.html (use the first URL that doesn't return 404) may be helpful. If you need access to the platform version of a file, you can retrieve it from the smc-upgrades remote in the release/{TO_VERSION} branch. The validation command is expected to take around 10 minutes to execute. After fixing issues in a module, use the -rf Maven option to continue the build from the failed module to save time.

steps.title: Resolve local database reset failures
steps.prompt: Run the validation command below to reset the local database and help to fix any failures. The upgrade notes at https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/{TO_VERSION}/upgrade-notes.html, https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/{TO_VERSION}/release-notes.html#upgrade-notes, or https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/upgrade-notes.html (use the first URL that doesn't return 404) may be helpful. steps.title: Resolve Integration Server startup issues
steps.prompt: Run the validation command below to start the Integration Server and help to fix any startup issues. You will need to have ActiveMQ running at the same time. It can be started using "mvn clean package cargo:run -f extensions/activemq-broker". The upgrade notes at https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/{TO_VERSION}/upgrade-notes.html, https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/{TO_VERSION}/release-notes.html#upgrade-notes, or https://documentation.elasticpath.com/commerce/docs/upgrade-notes.html (use the first URL that doesn't return 404) may be helpful. If you need access to the platform version of a file, you can retrieve it from the smc-upgrades remote in the release/{TO_VERSION} branch.
